is a [Group 1](/wiki/Conditions_races "Conditions races") [flat](/wiki/Flat_racing "Flat racing") [horse race](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") in Hong Kong which is open to [thoroughbreds](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred") aged three years or older. It is run each year in mid-December over a distance of 2,400 metres (about 1+1⁄2 miles or 12 furlongs) at [Sha Tin](/wiki/Sha_Tin_Racecourse "Sha Tin Racecourse").
* 2:24.77 – [Glory Vase](/wiki/Glory_Vase "Glory Vase") (2019)
* 2 – [Luso](/w/index.php?title=Luso_(horse)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Luso (horse) (page does not exist)") (1996, 1997)
* 4 – [Olivier Peslier](/wiki/Olivier_Peslier "Olivier Peslier") (1995, 1999, 2007, 2008)
* 3 – [Aidan O'Brien](/wiki/Aidan_O%27Brien "Aidan O'Brien") (2015, 2017, 2020)
* 3 – Derrick Smith, [Susan Magnier](/wiki/John_Magnier "John Magnier") & [Michael Tabor](/wiki/Michael_Tabor "Michael Tabor") (2015, 2017, 2020)

["Race Info – LONGINES Hong Kong Vase - LONGINES Hong Kong International Races 2023 - The Hong Kong Jockey Club"](https://racing.hkjc.com/racing/english/international-racing/pdf/2023-24-LONGINES-Hong-Kong-Vase.pdf) (PDF). Retrieved 2023-12-10.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Horse race

Longines Hong Kong Vase

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Class | Group 1 |
| Location | [Sha Tin Racecourse](/wiki/Sha_Tin_Racecourse "Sha Tin Racecourse") Hong Kong, China |
| Inaugurated | 1994 |
| Race type | [Flat](/wiki/Flat_racing "Flat racing") / [Thoroughbred](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred") |
| Sponsor | [Longines](/wiki/Longines "Longines") |
| Website | [Hong Kong Jockey Club](http://www.hkjc.com/English/events/index.htm) |
| Race information | |
| Distance | 2,400 metres (1.5 miles) |
| Surface | Turf |
| Track | Right-handed |
| Qualification | Three-years-old and up |
| Weight | 121 [lb](/wiki/Pound_(mass) "Pound (mass)") (3y); 126 lb (4y+) **Allowances** 4 lb for [fillies](/wiki/Filly "Filly") and [mares](/wiki/Mare "Mare") |
| Purse | [HK$](/wiki/Hong_Kong_dollar "Hong Kong dollar")24,000,000 *(2023)*[[1]](#cite_note-1) **1st:** HK$13,440,000 |

The **Hong Kong Vase** is a [Group 1](/wiki/Conditions_races "Conditions races") [flat](/wiki/Flat_racing "Flat racing") [horse race](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") in Hong Kong which is open to [thoroughbreds](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred") aged three years or older. It is run each year in mid-December over a distance of 2,400 metres (about 1+1⁄2 miles or 12 furlongs) at [Sha Tin](/wiki/Sha_Tin_Racecourse "Sha Tin Racecourse").

The race was first run in 1994, and was promoted to Group 1 status in 2000. It is one of the four [Hong Kong International Races](/wiki/Hong_Kong_International_Races "Hong Kong International Races"), presently offering a purse of HK$24,000,000 (approximately US$3 million).

## Records

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hong_Kong_Vase&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Records")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/54/HKIR_20231210_Hong_Kong_Vase_%28trophy%29.jpg/250px-HKIR_20231210_Hong_Kong_Vase_%28trophy%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:HKIR_20231210_Hong_Kong_Vase_(trophy).jpg)

Trophy of the Hong Kong Vase

**Speed record:**

* 2:24.77 – [Glory Vase](/wiki/Glory_Vase "Glory Vase") (2019)

**Most wins:**

* 2 – [Luso](/w/index.php?title=Luso_(horse)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Luso (horse) (page does not exist)") (1996, 1997)
* 2 – [Doctor Dino](/w/index.php?title=Doctor_Dino_(horse)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Doctor Dino (horse) (page does not exist)") (2007, 2008)
* 2 – [Highland Reel](/wiki/Highland_Reel "Highland Reel") (2015, 2017)
* 2 – [Glory Vase](/wiki/Glory_Vase "Glory Vase") (2019, 2021)

**Most wins by a [jockey](/wiki/Jockey "Jockey"):**

* 4 – [Olivier Peslier](/wiki/Olivier_Peslier "Olivier Peslier") (1995, 1999, 2007, 2008)

**Most wins by a [trainer](/wiki/Horse_trainer "Horse trainer"):**

* 3 – [Aidan O'Brien](/wiki/Aidan_O%27Brien "Aidan O'Brien") (2015, 2017, 2020)
* 3 – [André Fabre](/wiki/Andr%C3%A9_Fabre "André Fabre") (1999, 2014, 2023)

**Most wins by an owner:**

* 3 – Derrick Smith, [Susan Magnier](/wiki/John_Magnier "John Magnier") & [Michael Tabor](/wiki/Michael_Tabor "Michael Tabor") (2015, 2017, 2020)
